text: A flexible endoscope with a distal cap attachment is used to perform incisional therapy on a large Zenker's diverticulum. An insulated tip needle knife applies endocut current to the diverticulum tissue.

The surgeon performs a septotomy using an endoscope, making incisions through the mucosal and submucosal layers to expose the cricopharyngeal bar. The endoscopic myotomy continues down to the base of the Zenker's pouch, allowing the endoscope to pass smoothly into the proximal esophagus.
The surgeon observes a mucosal defect with mild bleeding following the myotomy. A Conmet dura clip is applied to close the septotomy defect in the upper esophagus.

This clip is easily rotatable with a one to one rotation even over the distal cap attachment and has an eleven millimeter jaw with a small tail making it very advantageous to use in tight positions such as this case. It has a one step easy deployment with a tactile feedback to the assistant.

Following the placement of the initial clip, the surgeon identifies a small residual defect. A second duraclip is rotated and positioned to close the remaining side of the septotomy defect, despite the tight quarters.
